translation.en: She seems to like eating fruit.

content.sentence: Cô ấy ___ thích ăn trái cây.

exampleSentences: Có vẻ hôm nay trời sẽ mưa, em nên mang theo ô.
Anh ấy có vẻ mệt nên anh sẽ về nhà nghỉ ngơi sớm.
Chị có vẻ thích món ăn này vì chị ăn rất nhiều.
notes:
có vẻ = seems / appears. You use it when something looks or feels a certain way, but you’re not 100% sure.

It goes before an adjective or verb phrase to show your impression.  

It’s softer and less direct than saying something as a fact. Close to hình như (seems like), but có vẻ is based more on what you observe.
exampleSentencesTranslated: It seems it will rain today, so you should bring an umbrella.
He seems tired so he will go home to rest early.
She seems to like this dish because she ate a lot.

example.en: He seems tired after a long day at work.
example.vi: Anh ấy có vẻ mệt sau một ngày làm việc dài.

definitions.en: Used to indicate that something appears to be true or seems to be the case.
definitions.vi: Diễn tả một điều gì đó có khả năng đúng hoặc có dấu hiệu như vậy.

notes_md:
**có vẻ** = *seems / appears*. You use it when something looks or feels a certain way, but you’re not 100% sure.

It goes before an adjective or verb phrase to show your impression.  

It’s softer and less direct than saying something as a fact. Close to **hình như** (seems like), but **có vẻ** is based more on what you observe.
